page: Microsoft Azure D8ps_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dpsv6 family, powered by the ARM64 architecture. It features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs utilizing the Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor running at 3.4 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 128 MB of L3 cache per core, with no hyperthreading. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable for remote disk attachments.

Performance benchmarks indicate average capabilities across most general compute workloads. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance scores 2075.09, while the multi-core score reaches 16570.32. Memory performance is a notable highlight, with the Passmark memory score ranking in the top tier (top 10%) and read-only small block memory bandwidth reaching 909.9 GB/sec. Single-threaded gzip compression performs strongly in the top 25% at 33.17 GB/sec. However, cryptographic performance is a relative weakness, with the Passmark CPU encryption test scoring 1504.14, placing it in the bottom 25% due to the lack of advanced vector and matrix extensions like AVX or AMX.

This server is designed for general-purpose workloads such as static web serving, which achieves a throughput of 15.84 GB/sec, and database operations, scoring an average of 9906.67 on Passmark. It is also capable of handling lightweight machine learning tasks, processing 444.87 tokens per second for a small 135M LLM model, though performance drops significantly to a poor rating of 12.34 tokens per second for prompt processing on medium 7B models. The absence of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, making it best suited for application servers, containerized microservices, and standard web applications that do not require high-speed local scratch space.
description: Microsoft Azure D8ps_v6 is an ARM64-based general-purpose virtual machine from the Dpsv6 family, running on a 3.4 GHz Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor with 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs. It features 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core, but lacks local storage and integrated GPUs. Benchmark results show average performance for most general compute tasks, with a strong showing in memory bandwidth and single-threaded gzip compression. However, cryptographic operations represent a performance tradeoff, scoring in the bottom 25% due to the absence of AVX and AMX extensions. This instance is well-positioned for workloads such as static web serving, database operations, and lightweight application hosting where dedicated ARM resources offer a balanced, cost-efficient profile without the need for high-speed local storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D8ps_v6 is an ARM64 general-purpose virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated Neoverse-N2 cores, 32 GB RAM, and top-tier memory bandwidth, suitable for web serving and database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D8ps_v6 is an ARM64 general-purpose VM with 8 dedicated cores, 32 GB RAM, and strong memory bandwidth for web and database tasks.
tagline: An ARM64-powered general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated cores, high memory bandwidth, and balanced resources for standard enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: ARM64 architecture with 8 dedicated Microsoft Neoverse-N2 cores running at 3.4 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per vCPU
Top-tier memory performance with 909.9 GB/sec small-block read bandwidth
Strong single-threaded gzip compression performance at 33.17 GB/sec
No local storage or integrated GPUs, requiring network-attached Premium Storage
Suited for static web serving, database operations, and containerized microservices
